svg.text: For three to four months, every single bit deviation meant drilling through all four layers

g.text: Layer 1 · The full ITU test-vector set
Input: thousands of .pcm speech files · each with its reference output bit-stream
Run codec → output bit-stream → compare bit-by-bit to reference → find the first failing vector
e.g. vector #347 starts deviating after frame #124
Layer 2 · Inside that failing vector · hundreds of frames
Each frame = 20ms of speech · dozens of intermediate states: LPC coefficients + excitation + quantizer indices
dump every frame's intermediate state → compare to reference → find the first deviating frame
e.g. frame #124 · LPC coefficient a[3] off by 1 LSB
Layer 3 · Inside that frame · dozens of inner loops · hundreds of multiply-accumulates each
ACELP algebraic codebook search · LPC analysis · pitch tracker, and other inner loops
dump every loop iteration's accumulator → compare to reference → find the first deviating iteration
e.g. algebraic codebook search · iteration #17 · 40-bit accumulator off by 1
Layer 4 · Inside that one multiply-accumulate · a basic operator's corner case
L_mac, L_msu, mult_r, saturate, L_shl … dozens of ITU-defined basic operators
check that operation's boundary condition → find the root of the 1 LSB difference → fix
e.g. mult_r on 0x7FFF × 0x7FFF + round: my assembly saturated one step earlier than the reference

svg.text: Every bit deviation = four layers down · nearly all of three-to-four months lived on this loop
